courses.title: Introduction to Cognitive Science
courses.description: Introduces the central questions, theories, and research methods of cognitive science — the interdisciplinary study of how minds work in humans, animals, and machines. Draws on philosophy, psychology, linguistics, neuroscience, and computer science to explore perception, memory, reasoning, and language.
courses.verification_note: Prerequisites and exclusions not explicitly listed on calendar page; marked empty pending verification.

courses.title: Foundations of Computer Science I
courses.description: Introductory computer science blending programming in Python with mathematical analysis and formal reasoning. Covers data representations, program control flow, mathematical logic and proof, numerical computation, algorithm analysis, and software engineering principles. No prior programming experience required.
courses.verification_note: Restricted to students in the first-year Computer Science admission stream. Offered only in Fall term.

courses.title: Foundations of Computer Science II
courses.description: Continues from CSC110Y1, extending programming and mathematical analysis. Covers object-oriented programming, binary representation, recursion and induction, abstract data types and data structures (stacks, queues, linked lists, trees, graphs), and the limitations of computation.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: CSC110Y1 with a grade of 70% or higher.
courses.verification_note: Restricted to students in the first-year Computer Science admission stream. Offered only in Winter term.

courses.title: Introduction to Computer Programming
courses.description: Programming in Python covering elementary data types, lists, maps, control flow, functions, classes, objects, methods, algorithms and problem solving, searching, sorting, and complexity. Also covers unit testing, floating-point numbers, and numerical computation. No prior programming experience required.

courses.title: Introduction to Computer Science
courses.description: Abstract data types and implementing data structures, linked data structures, encapsulation and information-hiding, object-oriented programming, specifications, efficiency analysis, and recursion. Assumes programming experience as provided by CSC108H1.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: CSC108H1 or equivalent programming experience.

courses.title: Calculus I for Life Sciences
courses.description: Introduction to differential calculus motivated by the problem of measuring how quantities change. Emphasizes deep conceptual understanding and translating between algebraic, graphical, numerical, and verbal representations. Covers limits, continuity, derivatives, linear approximation, differential equations, slope fields, and Euler's method, with applications to life sciences.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: High school level calculus.

courses.title: Calculus I
courses.description: Introduction to differential calculus centered on the problem of measuring how quantities change. Emphasizes deep conceptual understanding of why calculus tools work and how to apply them across social, biological, and physical sciences. Covers limits, continuity, derivatives, linear approximation, differential equations, slope fields, and Euler's method.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: High school level calculus.

courses.title: Calculus II
courses.description: Second-semester calculus focused on integral calculus. Begins with the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus connecting differentiation and integration. Covers integration techniques, improper integrals, Taylor polynomials and series, power series, and first-order differential equations. Builds on translating between algebraic, graphical, numerical, and verbal descriptions.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: MAT130H1 / MAT135H1 / MAT135H5 / MATA30H3 / MATA31H3 / APS162H1 / ESC194H1 / MAT186H1 / MAT196H1 (one of). Multiple alternative first calculus courses are accepted.

courses.title: Calculus I with Proofs
courses.description: Conceptual approach to calculus for students with theoretical math interests. Covers logic and quantifiers, sets, mathematical induction, limits and continuity, the mean value theorem and applications, elementary transcendental functions, the inverse function theorem, and differentiation with applications.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: High school level calculus.
courses.verification_note: Replaces the former full-year MAT137Y1 as a half-course equivalent (together with MAT149H1).

courses.title: Calculus II with Proofs
courses.description: Continuation of calculus with proofs. Covers integration and applications, the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus, improper integrals, sequences and series, power series, and Taylor's theorem and applications. Conceptually oriented for students pursuing theoretical math foundations.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: MAT148H1 / MAT135H1 (70%+) / MAT130H1 (70%+) / MAT158H1 (one of). Multiple entry paths accepted depending on background.
courses.verification_note: Replaces the former full-year MAT137Y1 as a half-course equivalent (together with MAT148H1).

courses.title: Calculus with Proofs
courses.description: Full-year calculus sequence with an emphasis on mathematical rigour and proofs. Covers differential and integral calculus in depth.
courses.verification_note: NOT FOUND in the current UofT Arts & Science Academic Calendar (2025-2026). This course appears in the official ASMAJ1446A program requirement text but may have been retired. The current calendar lists MAT148H1 (Calculus I with Proofs) + MAT149H1 (Calculus II with Proofs) as the replacement half-course equivalents. Students should verify this pathway option with the official Academic Calendar or academic advising before planning.

courses.title: Analysis 1A
courses.description: Theoretical calculus course emphasizing proofs and techniques. Covers elementary logic, limits and continuity, least upper bounds, intermediate and extreme value theorems, derivatives, the mean value and inverse function theorems, integrals, the Fundamental Theorem, and elementary transcendental functions.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: High school level calculus.
courses.verification_note: Replaces the former full-year MAT157Y1 as a half-course equivalent (together with MAT159H1). Required for Mathematics Specialist programs.

courses.title: Analysis 1B
courses.description: Continuation of theoretical calculus emphasizing proofs and techniques. Covers integrals, the Fundamental Theorem, techniques of integration, elementary transcendental functions, Taylor's theorem, series, and uniform convergence with power series.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: MAT158H1 / MAT148H1 (70%+) / MAT157H5 (one of).
courses.verification_note: Replaces the former full-year MAT157Y1 as a half-course equivalent (together with MAT158H1).

courses.title: Analysis I
courses.description: Rigorous introduction to real analysis including axioms for the real numbers, sequences, limits, continuity, differentiation, integration, and series.
courses.verification_note: NOT FOUND in the current UofT Arts & Science Academic Calendar (2025-2026). This course appears in the official ASMAJ1446A program requirement text but may have been retired. The current calendar lists MAT158H1 (Analysis 1A) + MAT159H1 (Analysis 1B) as the replacement half-course equivalents. Students should verify this pathway option with the official Academic Calendar or academic advising before planning.

courses.title: Mathematical Expression and Reasoning for Computer Science
courses.description: Introduction to abstraction, rigour, and logical notation and reasoning. Covers precise mathematical expressions, structuring proofs, general problem-solving, floating-point representation, running time analysis of iterative programs, Big-Oh notation, diagonalization, and the Halting Problem with reductions.
courses.prerequisite_note: Corequisite: CSC108H1 / CSC120H1 / equivalent programming experience.

courses.title: Foundations of Cognitive Science
courses.description: Covers major approaches and domains in cognitive science beyond introductory material, including perception and attention, language and cognition, thinking and reasoning, computational models of cognition, and the mind-brain relationship. Emphasizes integrating insights from philosophy, psychology, linguistics, neuroscience, and computer science.

courses.title: The Practice of Statistics I
courses.description: Introductory statistics covering exploratory data analysis for univariate and bivariate data, sampling and experimental designs, basic probability models, and estimation and hypothesis testing for one-sample and two-sample comparative studies. A statistical computing package is used; no prior computing experience required.
courses.verification_note: STA220H1 does not count as a distribution requirement course. Recommended preparation: Grade 12 Mathematics and one university course in BR3/BR4/BR5.

courses.title: Probability, Statistics and Data Analysis I
courses.description: Introduction to probability blending simulation with mathematical frameworks for statistical practice. Covers probability spaces, random variables, discrete and continuous distributions, expectation and variance, independence, conditional probability, the law of large numbers, the central limit theorem, and sampling distributions. Computer simulation used extensively.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: any standard first-year calculus stream (e.g., MAT135H1+MAT136H1, MAT137Y1, MAT157Y1, or UTM/UTSC equivalents).
courses.corequisite_note: Official corequisite: CSC108H1 / CSC110Y1 / CSC148H1 or equivalent programming experience. May be completed concurrently or in advance.

courses.title: Probability, Statistics and Data Analysis II
courses.description: Statistical inference foundations including statistical models, parameter estimators and their properties, estimation methods, confidence intervals, hypothesis testing, likelihood functions, and the linear model. Includes statistical computation for data analysis and simulation.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: STA237H1 / STA247H1 / STA257H1 / STAB52H3 / STA256H5 (one of).
courses.corequisite_note: Official corequisite: CSC108H1 / CSC110Y1 / CSC148H1 or equivalent. May be completed concurrently or in advance.

courses.title: Probability with Computer Applications
courses.description: Introduction to probability through simulation and mathematical approaches, focused on topics relevant to computer science and statistical practice. Covers probability spaces, random variables, distributions, expectation and variance, independence, conditional probability, the law of large numbers, the central limit theorem, and sampling distributions.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites require BOTH a math component AND a CS component. Math (one of): (MAT130H1/MAT135H1, MAT136H1) / (MAT148H1, MAT149H1) / MAT137Y1 / (MAT158H1, MAT159H1) / MAT157Y1 / UTM/UTSC equivalents. CS (one of): CSC111H1 / CSC148H1 / CSCA48H3 / CSC148H5.

courses.title: Statistics for Computer Scientists
courses.description: Statistical inference and practice designed for computer science applications. Covers statistical models and parameters, estimators and their properties, estimation methods, confidence intervals, hypothesis testing, the likelihood function, and the linear model. Includes statistical computation for data analysis and simulation.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites require BOTH a stats component AND a CS component. Stats (one of): STA237H1 / STA247H1 / STA257H1 / STA256H5 / STAB52H3. CS (one of): CSC111H1 / CSC148H1 / CSCA48H3 / CSC148H5.

courses.title: Statistical Theory
courses.description: Mathematical treatment of probability distributions (discrete and continuous), conditional probability, expectation, sampling distributions, estimation and testing, and the linear model. References material discussed in STA220H1.
courses.verification_note: STA255H1 does not count as a distribution requirement course.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites: one statistics course (STA220H1 / STA221H1 / STA288H1 / ECO220Y1 / STAB22H3 / STA220H5 / ECO220Y5) AND one calculus sequence (MAT133Y1 with 70% / MAT135H1+MAT136H1 / MAT137Y1 / MAT157Y1 / UTM/UTSC equivalents; some require 70%+).

courses.title: Probability and Statistics I
courses.description: Mathematically rigorous introduction to probability with applications to statistical inference. Covers probability and expectation, discrete and continuous random variables and vectors, distribution and density functions, the law of large numbers, binomial, geometric, Poisson, and normal distributions, and the Central Limit Theorem.
courses.verification_note: STA257H1 does not count as a distribution requirement course.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite requires a theoretical calculus background: MAT148H1+MAT149H1 / MAT137Y1 / MAT158H1+MAT159H1 / MAT157Y1 / UTM/UTSC equivalents (one of). MAT135H1+MAT136H1 is NOT sufficient.
courses.corequisite_note: Official corequisites are TWO components: (1) MAT237Y1 / MAT257Y1 / MAT257Y5; AND (2) MAT223H1 / MAT224H1 / MAT240H1 / MATA22H3 / MATA23H3 / MAT223H5 / MAT240H5 / MATB24H3 / MAT224H5.

courses.title: Statistics I
courses.description: Fundamentals of descriptive and inferential statistics, including population and sampling distributions, simple association, probability, estimation, and hypothesis testing.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: PSY100H1 / PSY100Y5 / (PSYA01H3 AND PSYA02H3) / COG250Y1 (one of). Recommended preparation: Grade 12 Calculus.

courses.title: Introduction to Cognitive Psychology
courses.description: Research and theory on the neural and cognitive architecture of attention, memory, language, thinking, and reasoning.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: PSY100H1 / PSY100Y5 / PSYA01H3 / COG250Y1 (one of).

courses.title: Behavioural Neuroscience
courses.description: Biological underpinnings of behaviour including functional neuroanatomy, neural signalling, sensory and motor processes, and hormones. Draws on both animal and human research.

courses.title: Minds and Machines
courses.description: Philosophical issues in cognitive science and AI, including machine consciousness, the computational theory of mind, embodied cognition, and the ethics of machine learning.

courses.title: Data, Computation, and the Mind
courses.description: Explores how the human mind works by analyzing data on topics such as rationality and irrationality, human memory, mental object representation, and the language-cognition relationship. Teaches critical thinking and practical computational skills for working with data in cognitive science and neighboring fields.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: CSC108H1 / CSC148H1 (one of).
courses.corequisite_note: Official corequisite: COG250Y1. COG250Y1 was replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1. Students must have completed or be concurrently enrolled in both.
courses.verification_note: Corequisite references the former COG250Y1 (full-year course), now replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1.

courses.title: Issues in Cognitive Science I: Attention, Perception, and Consciousness
courses.description: Examination of core topics in cognitive science building on foundational coursework. Typical topics include perception and attention, concepts, imagery, and consciousness.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: COG250Y1 AND one of PSY270H1 / PHL342H1. COG250Y1 was replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1. The second component (PSY270H1 or PHL342H1) must also be satisfied.

courses.title: Issues in Cognitive Science II: Concepts, Theories of Mind, and Cognitive Evolution
courses.description: Examination of core topics in cognitive science building on foundational coursework. Typical topics include concepts, theories of mind, and cognitive evolution.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: COG250Y1 AND one of PSY270H1 / PHL342H1. COG250Y1 was replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1. The second component (PSY270H1 or PHL342H1) must also be satisfied.

courses.title: Issues in Cognitive Science IV: Language and Communication
courses.description: Examination of core topics in cognitive science building on foundational coursework. Typical topics include language and cognition, language acquisition, theories of meaning, and pragmatics.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: COG250Y1 AND one of LIN232H1 / LIN241H1 or JLP315H1 / JLP374H1. COG250Y1 was replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1. The second component requires one linguistics or psycholinguistics course from the listed options.

courses.title: Issues in Cognitive Science V: Cognitive Science and Society
courses.description: Examination of core topics in cognitive science building on foundational coursework. Topics include central moral, social, and political questions both relevant to and raised by cognitive science research.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: COG250Y1 AND one of PSY270H1 / PHL342H1. COG250Y1 was replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1. The second component (PSY270H1 or PHL342H1) must also be satisfied.

courses.title: Cognitive Science in Practice
courses.description: Provides Cognitive Science students with opportunities to practice quantitative and qualitative evaluation and assessment methods in real-world situations, juxtaposing theory with practice. Placement activities ground topics such as problem solving, logistics, and decision making through practical application and individual and group critical reflection.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites: COG250Y1 (replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1); AND COG341H1 / COG342H1 (one of); AND PSY370H1.
courses.verification_note: Winter offering. Small enrollment (~23 students). 18L/15P format. No TA support.

courses.title: Independent Study
courses.description: Advanced independent study course. Requires a written proposal and faculty supervisor approval through the UC Programs Office for the Program Director's approval. Applications are due before course enrolment begins. Not eligible for CR/NCR.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: 15.0 credits completed AND a minimum cGPA of 3.0.
courses.verification_note: Breadth requirement not listed on Academic Calendar page.

courses.title: Individual Study in Cognitive Science
courses.description: Advanced independent study course. Requires a written proposal and faculty supervisor approval through the UC Programs Office for the Program Director's approval. Applications are due before the first day of course enrolment. Not eligible for CR/NCR.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: 15.0 credits completed AND a minimum cGPA of 3.0.
courses.verification_note: Breadth requirement not listed on Academic Calendar page.

courses.title: Methods and Analysis in Behavioral Neuroscience
courses.description: Bridges biology and computational analysis using example-based approaches in behavioral neuroscience contexts like sleep, movement, learning, and decision-making. Covers modern neuroscience methodology (electrophysiology, optogenetics, calcium imaging) and neural data analysis. Topics include MATLAB programming for neuroscience, signal processing, image processing, statistical analysis, and machine learning applied to EEG, EMG, and calcium imaging data.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites: BIO270H1 AND BIO271H1 (or PSL300H1 + PSL301H1) AND MAT136H1. Multiple components required. Recommended: familiarity with linear algebra, CSB280H1, CJH332H1.

courses.title: Software Design
courses.description: Software design and development concepts, methods, and tools using a statically-typed object-oriented language. Topics include version control, unit testing, refactoring, design patterns, and regular expressions.

courses.title: Computational Principles and Methods for Data Science
courses.description: Builds on introductory data science and programming skills. Covers gathering and storing data via web APIs and scraping; data preprocessing for data science algorithms; version control for reproducible workflows; web tools for communicating results; and creating, distributing, and accessing open-source data science libraries.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: CSC108H1 / CSC108H5 / CSCA08H3 / CSC110Y1 / CSC148H1 / CSC148H5 / CSCA48H3 (one of).
courses.verification_note: Recommended preparation: EEB125H1 / GGR274H1 / ENG286H1 / STA130H1.

courses.title: Social and Information Networks
courses.description: How networks underlie the social, technological, and natural worlds, with an emphasis on broadly applicable concepts in network analysis. Covers graph theory, network concepts, game theory, social networks, information networks, aggregate behavior of markets and crowds, network dynamics, and information diffusion.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites require THREE components: (1) CSC263H1 / CSC265H1 / CSC263H5 / CSCB63H3; (2) STA247H1 / STA255H1 / STA257H1 / ECO227Y1 / STA237H1 / STAB52H3 / STAB57H3; (3) MAT223H1 / MAT240H1.
courses.verification_note: Non-CS Major/Specialist or non-Data Science Specialist students are limited to a maximum of 1.5 credits in 300-/400-level CSC/ECE courses.

courses.title: Algorithmic Game Theory and Mechanism Design
courses.description: Mathematical and computational introduction to game theory and mechanism design. Covers analysis of equilibria in games and computation of price of anarchy; design and analysis of mechanisms with monetary transfers such as auctions; and mechanisms without monetary transfers such as voting and matching. Intended for economics, mathematics, and computer science students.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites: (1) STA247H1 / STA255H1 / STA257H1 / STA237H1 / PSY201H1 / ECO227Y1; AND (2) a calculus sequence: (MAT130H1 / MAT135H1 / MAT148H1 / MAT158H1, MAT136H1 / MAT149H1 / MAT159H1) / MAT137Y1 / MAT157Y1. Multiple calculus pathways accepted.
courses.verification_note: Recommended preparation: MAT223H1, CSC373H1. Non-CS Major/Specialist or non-Data Science Specialist students are limited to a maximum of 1.5 credits in 300-/400-level CSC/ECE courses.

courses.title: Introduction to Machine Learning
courses.description: Methods for automated learning of relationships on the basis of empirical data. Topics include classification and regression via nearest neighbour methods, decision trees, linear models, and neural networks, as well as clustering algorithms and overfitting.

courses.title: Introduction to Visual Computing
courses.description: Image acquisition and computational processing of 2D images for students interested in computer vision, digital photography, and computer graphics. Covers mathematical and engineering foundations, low-level computer vision algorithms (warping, morphing, stitching, enhancement, inpainting, color processing, face recognition), and turning algorithmic descriptions into working Python code.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites: CSC263H1 / CSC265H1 / CSC263H5 / CSCB63H3; AND MAT223H1 / MAT240H1; AND one of: MAT136H1 (77%+) / MAT149H1 (73%+) / MAT137Y1 (73%+) / MAT159H1 (67%+) / MAT157Y1 (67%+) / MAT235H1 / MAT235Y1 / MAT237Y1 / MAT257Y1. Grade thresholds apply to specific calculus courses.
courses.verification_note: Recommended: MAT235H1 / MAT235Y1 / MAT237Y1 / MAT257Y1. Non-CS Major/Specialist or non-Data Science Specialist students are limited to a maximum of 1.5 credits in 300-/400-level CSC/ECE courses.

courses.title: Principles of Programming Languages
courses.description: Programming principles common in modern languages and details of commonly used paradigms. Covers structure and meaning of code, scope, control flow, datatypes, and parameter passing. Two non-procedural, non-object-oriented paradigms are taught in depth: functional programming and logic programming.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: CSC263H1 / CSC265H1 / CSC263H5 / CSCB63H3 (one of).
courses.verification_note: Non-CS Major/Specialist or non-Data Science Specialist students are limited to a maximum of 1.5 credits in 300-/400-level CSC/ECE courses.

courses.title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence
courses.description: Core theories and algorithms of computational intelligence, including search, logical reasoning, automated planning, and decision making under uncertainty.

courses.title: Natural Language Computing
courses.description: Introduction to techniques involving natural language processing and speech in applications such as information retrieval, text classification, language modeling, and dialogue systems.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites require TWO components: (1) CSC207H1 / CSC209H1 / CSC207H5 / CSCB07H3 / CSC209H5 / CSCB09H3; AND (2) STA237H1 / STA247H1 / STA255H1 / STA257H1 / STAB57H3 / STAB52H3.
courses.verification_note: Recommended: CSC311H1. MAT223H1 / MAT240H1 is strongly recommended. Non-CS Major/Specialist or non-Data Science Specialist students are limited to a maximum of 1.5 credits in 300-/400-level CSC/ECE courses.

courses.title: Probabilistic Learning and Reasoning
courses.description: Introduction to probability as a means of representing and reasoning with uncertain knowledge. Covers qualitative and quantitative specification of probability distributions via probabilistic graphical models, inference and reasoning algorithms, statistical approaches for learning models from data, and applications in AI and machine learning.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: CSC311H1 / STA314H1 / CSCC11H3 / CSC311H5 (one of).
courses.verification_note: Non-CS Major/Specialist or non-Data Science Specialist students are limited to a maximum of 1.5 credits in 300-/400-level CSC/ECE courses.

courses.title: Neural Networks and Deep Learning
courses.description: Introduction to neural networks and deep learning. Covers backpropagation and automatic differentiation; architectures including convolutional networks and recurrent neural networks; methods for improving optimization and generalization; and neural networks for unsupervised and reinforcement learning.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites require THREE components: (1) CSC311H1 / CSC311H5 / CSCC11H3 / STA314H1; (2) multivariable calculus: MAT235H1 / MAT235Y1 / MAT237Y1 / MAT257Y1 / MAT232H5 / MAT233H5 / MATB41H3; (3) linear algebra: MAT223H1 / MAT240H1 / MAT223H5 / MATA23H3.
courses.verification_note: Non-CS Major/Specialist or non-Data Science Specialist students are limited to a maximum of 1.5 credits in 300-/400-level CSC/ECE courses.

courses.title: Introduction to Image Understanding
courses.description: Introduction to basic concepts in computer vision. Covers extraction of image features at multiple scales, robust estimation, multiview geometry, image motion estimation and tracking, object recognition, and scene understanding.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites require THREE components: (1) CSC263H1 / CSC265H1 / CSC263H5 / CSCB63H3 / ECE345H1 / ECE358H1 / MIE245H1; (2) CSC311H1 / STA314H1 / CSC311H5 / CSCC11H3 / ROB313H1 / ECE421H1; (3) CSC320H1 / CSC320H5.
courses.verification_note: Non-CS Major/Specialist or non-Data Science Specialist students are limited to a maximum of 1.5 credits in 300-/400-level CSC/ECE courses.

courses.title: Computational Linguistics
courses.description: Computational linguistics and the processing of language by computer. Covers context-free grammars, chart parsing, statistical parsing, semantics and semantic interpretation, ambiguity resolution techniques, and reference resolution. Emphasizes statistical learning methods for lexical, syntactic, and semantic knowledge.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites require TWO components: (1) CSC209H1 / CSC209H5 / CSCB09H3; AND (2) STA237H1 / STA247H1 / STA255H1 / STA257H1.
courses.verification_note: Recommended: CSC311H1, CSC324H1 / CSC384H1. 36L hours (more than typical 24L). Non-CS Major/Specialist or non-Data Science Specialist students are limited to a maximum of 1.5 credits in 300-/400-level CSC/ECE courses.

courses.title: Knowledge Representation and Reasoning
courses.description: Representing knowledge symbolically in a form suitable for automated reasoning, and associated reasoning methods. Covers first-order logic, entailment, the resolution method, Horn clauses, procedural representations, production systems, description logics, inheritance networks, defaults and probabilities, tractable reasoning, abductive explanation, the representation of action, and planning.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: CSC384H1 / CSC384H5 / CSCD84H3 (one of).
courses.verification_note: Non-CS Major/Specialist or non-Data Science Specialist students are limited to a maximum of 1.5 credits in 300-/400-level CSC/ECE courses.

courses.title: Psycholinguistics: Language Acquisition in Children
courses.description: Examines how children acquire language from birth through theory and experiments. Covers infants' abilities at birth, cognitive development, word-learning, phonological, syntactic and semantic development, and conversational abilities. Students work with behavioral methods and child language corpora.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: 1.0 credit at the 200+ level in LIN / JAL / JUP / PSL / PSY / COG. Broad prerequisite allowing multiple entry paths. 36L format. Sponsored by Linguistics and Psychology, administered by Linguistics.

courses.title: Psycholinguistics: Language Processing in Adults
courses.description: Explores how the human mind learns, processes, understands, and produces language. Combines theory with experimental evidence covering word interpretation, sentence planning and articulation, social context in language, and cross-linguistic similarities and differences. Students engage with behavioral methods and eye-tracking research.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: 1.0 credit from LIN228H1 / LIN229H1 / LIN232H1 / LIN241H1 / PSY260H1 / PSY270H1 / PSY280H1 / PSY290H1 / COG250Y1. COG250Y1 replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1. 36L format. Sponsored by Linguistics and Psychology, administered by Linguistics.

courses.title: Advanced Psycholinguistics
courses.description: Seminar in advanced topics in psycholinguistics. Content varies from year to year. Sponsored by Linguistics and Psychology, administered solely by Linguistics.
courses.verification_note: 24S seminar format. Content varies by year.

courses.title: Introduction to Linguistics: Sentence Structure and Meaning
courses.description: Foundational linguistics principles focusing on sentence and meaning structure, including acquisition, processing, and variation. Practice in elementary analytic techniques applied to data from a wide range of languages.

courses.title: Phonetics
courses.description: Investigates the sounds most commonly used in languages from an articulatory and acoustic point of view, with practical work in sound recognition and production. Covers the International Phonetic Alphabet (IPA) and transcription of diverse speech sounds.
courses.verification_note: Recommended preparation: LIN101H1 / LIN200H1.

courses.title: Syntactic Patterns in Language
courses.description: The nature and organization of syntactic systems; their relation to semantic systems and the linguistic organization of discourse. Practical work in linguistic analysis.
courses.verification_note: Breadth Requirement is not listed on the Academic Calendar page; confirmed UNKNOWN.

courses.title: Introduction to Semantics
courses.description: Introduction to meaning within linguistics and the interpretation of language in context. Covers logical and semantic relations, pragmatic concepts such as presupposition and implicature, thematic roles, quantifiers and scope, and expressions of temporal and modal relations in natural language.

courses.title: Acoustic Phonetics
courses.description: Introduction to the acoustic phonetics of the vocal tract, covering acoustic characteristics of speech and instrumental analysis methods.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites: LIN101H1 AND LIN102H1 AND LIN228H1. All three must be satisfied.

courses.title: Syntactic Theory
courses.description: Syntactic theory and analysis within a current formal framework. Students aiming for graduate studies in linguistics are strongly advised to include this course in their program.
courses.verification_note: 36L format.

courses.title: Semantic Theory
courses.description: Natural language semantics and how interpretation relates to syntactic structure. Topics include predication and quantification, scope and anaphora, problems of discourse analysis, pronoun interpretation, and ellipsis.
courses.verification_note: 36L format.

courses.title: Buddhism and Cognitive Science
courses.description: Interdisciplinary exploration of how cognitive science may connect with Buddhist terms, concepts, and practices, covering topics like wisdom, mindfulness, meditation, insight, self-control, flow, or mystical experience.
courses.verification_note: Previously numbered NEW333H1. Recommended preparation: BPM232H1 / NEW232Y1 / RLG206H1. 36L format.

courses.title: Mindfulness Meditation: Science and Research
courses.description: Examines the theoretical and empirical foundations of mindfulness-based interventions in healthcare and beyond. Critically addresses the roots of mindfulness, current models and adaptations, and applications, interventions, and outcomes. Reviews both quantitative and qualitative research methodologies along with limitations in the scientific literature. Builds scientific literacy skills through assessment, critique, and discussion of peer-reviewed journal articles.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: a statistics course (e.g., PSY201H1 / SOC202H1 / STA220H1).
courses.verification_note: Previously numbered NEW438H1. Recommended preparation: BPM232H1 / NEW232Y1 / BPM333H1. 36S seminar format.

courses.title: Persons, Minds and Bodies
courses.description: Consciousness and its relation to the body; personal identity and survival; knowledge of other minds; psychological events and behaviour.

courses.title: Modern Symbolic Logic
courses.description: Formal deductive logic covering semantics, symbolization, and natural deduction in sentential logic; monadic predicate logic with symbolization, natural deduction, and models; polyadic predicates with symbolization and natural deduction; plus an introduction to advanced first-order logic concepts such as operations, identity, and models.
courses.verification_note: 36L format.

courses.title: Probability and Inductive Logic
courses.description: Axiomatic probability theory and its main interpretations — frequency, logical, and subjective — along with reasoning with probabilities in decision-making and science.
courses.verification_note: Recommended preparation: PHL245H1.

courses.title: Intermediate Logic
courses.description: Builds on PHL245H1. Covers first-order logic together with metalogical results like soundness and completeness. Introduces basic set theory and metalogic. Possible additional topics include the Löwenheim-Skolem theorems and Gödel's incompleteness theorems.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: PHL245H1 / (MAT158H1, MAT159H1) / MAT157Y1 AND a full course in PHL / CSC / MAT. 36L format.

courses.title: Modal Logic and Philosophical Applications of Logic
courses.description: Formal study of necessity and possibility, modal propositional and quantificational logic, possible-worlds semantics, and modal metaphysics. Additional potential topics include counterfactuals, truth, vagueness, epistemic logic, temporal logic, or non-classical logic.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: PHL245H1 / (MAT158H1, MAT159H1) / MAT157Y1 AND a full course in PHL / CSC / MAT. Multiple entry paths for the logic component. 36L format. Recommended preparation: PHL345H1.

courses.title: Logic and Computation
courses.description: Covers Gödel's incompleteness theorems, the technical methods behind proving them, their link to computation theory, and their philosophical significance. Addresses questions such as whether there are unknowable mathematical truths and whether formal modeling has inherent limits.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: PHL245H1 / (MAT158H1, MAT159H1) / MAT157Y1 AND a full course in PHL / CSC / MAT. Multiple entry paths for the logic component. 36L format.

courses.title: Set Theory
courses.description: Introductory course on set theory highlighting its philosophical significance as a unifying structure for mathematics and logic. Covers paradoxes of naïve set theory, axiomatic resolution, construction of number systems within set theory, equivalents of the axiom of choice, and model theory.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: PHL245H1 / (MAT158H1, MAT159H1) / MAT157Y1 AND a full course in PHL / CSC / MAT. 36L format.

courses.title: Philosophy of Natural Science
courses.description: The structure and methods of science: explanation, methodology, realism and instrumentalism.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites: 7.5 credits which include one of: (1) PHL255H1 and an additional 1.0 PHL credit, OR (2) 1.5 PHL credits and HPS250H1. Complex prerequisite with multiple paths. 36L format.

courses.title: Understanding Neural Networks using Physics
courses.description: Examines neural network architectures and algorithms through a physics lens, employing analytical and numerical methods. Covers classification and regression with fully-connected networks, physics-based optimizers and activation functions, generative models (normalizing flows, diffusion models), and architectures such as residual networks and transformers.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites: MAT223H1/MAT240H1; MAT235Y1/MAT237Y1/MAT257Y1; PHY224H1; PHY252H1; PHY254H1. Five distinct course requirements — heavy physics and math background needed. Recommended: familiarity with Python and Jupyter. 24L/12T format.

courses.title: Introduction to Developmental Psychology
courses.description: The developmental approach to the study of behaviour with a focus on infancy through childhood. Topics include sensorimotor skills, cognition, socialization, personality, and emotional behaviour.

courses.title: Introduction to Social Psychology
courses.description: Contemporary areas of research in social psychology: social perception, attitudes, interpersonal relations, and group processes.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: PSY100H1 / PSY100Y5 / PSYA02H3 / COG250Y1 (one of). COG250Y1 replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1.

courses.title: Personality and Its Transformations
courses.description: Theory and research in personality structure and dynamics: the interaction of cultural and biological factors in the development and expression of individual differences.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: PSY100H1 / PSY100Y5 / PSYA02H3 / COG250Y1 (one of). COG250Y1 replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1.

courses.title: Introduction to Learning and Plasticity
courses.description: Classical and contemporary learning theories, the physiological and anatomical basis of learning and memory, synaptic plasticity, and applications to drug addiction, phobias, and other disorders.

courses.title: Introduction to Sensation and Perception
courses.description: Physiological and psychological basis of perception across sensory modalities, with emphasis on vision. Covers visual perception of shape, objects, scenes, colour, space, and motion; auditory perception of simple and complex sounds and location; and may include touch, chemical senses, and cross-modal influences.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: PSY100H1 / PSY100Y5 / PSYA01H3 / COG250Y1 (one of). COG250Y1 replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1.

courses.title: Cognitive Development
courses.description: Examines knowledge development in domains like spatial perception, navigation, object perception, number, language, and theory of mind. Focuses on the origin and nature of human knowledge.

courses.title: The Treatment of Psychological Data
courses.description: Practical yet intensive introduction to the research pipeline, with a focus on research data management and advanced statistical analysis and inference. Students learn to find, organize, and analyze datasets in a transparent and reproducible manner, with emphasis on how study design and data analysis shape result interpretation.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: PSY202H1 / ECO220Y1 / SOC252H1 / STA221H1 / ECO220Y5 / PSY202H5 / STA221H5 / PSYB06H3 / PSYC08H3 / STAB27H3 / STAC32H3 (one of). NOTE: Requires a second statistics course (PSY202H1 level), not the first statistics course (PSY201H1 level). The program statistics choice provides PSY201H1 level only; an additional statistics course would be needed.

courses.title: Perceptual Development
courses.description: Examines human perceptual development during the first 2-3 years of life. Vision and audition are emphasized.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites require TWO components: (1) a statistics course (PSY201H1 / ECO220Y1 / STA220H1 / STA238H1 / STA248H1 / STA288H1 or equivalents); AND (2) PSY280H1 / PSY280H5 / PSYB51H3. Recommended preparation: PSY210H1.

courses.title: Psychological Measurement
courses.description: Development and evaluation of psychological measures, including the measurement of knowledge, abilities, attitudes, and personality traits. Covers theoretical and methodological issues, reliability and validity, interpretation of test scores and research findings, and applications and ethics of psychological assessment.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: a statistics course (PSY201H1 / ECO220Y1 / STA220H1 / STA238H1 / STA248H1 / STA288H1 or equivalents). Recommended preparation: PSY202H1 (second statistics) and PSY230H1.
courses.verification_note: Breadth Requirement is not listed on the Academic Calendar page; confirmed UNKNOWN.

courses.title: Social Psychology of Emotion
courses.description: In-depth review of the role of emotion in human psychology, with focus on emotion-cognition links. Covers theories of emotion, emotion regulation, emotional expression and experience, emotion's role in decision-making, and connections among emotion, motivation, and behaviour.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites require TWO components: (1) a statistics course (PSY201H1 / ECO220Y1 / STA220H1 / STA238H1 / STA248H1 / STA288H1 or equivalents); AND (2) PSY220H1 / PSY220H5 / PSYB10H3 / SOC213H1.

courses.title: Animal Cognition
courses.description: The study of memory, representation, concept learning, and other cognitive processes in non-human animals.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites require TWO components: (1) a statistics course (PSY201H1 / ECO220Y1 / STA220H1 / STA238H1 / STA248H1 / STA288H1 or equivalents); AND (2) PSY260H1 / PSYB38H3 OR (PSY270H1 + PSY290H1 or equivalents — both required together for this option).

courses.title: Thinking and Reasoning
courses.description: Problem-solving as a model of directed thinking; conceptual behaviour and mental representation; induction, deduction and learning; probabilistic reasoning; creative thinking and complex problem solving.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites require TWO components: (1) a statistics course (PSY201H1 / ECO220Y1 / STA220H1 / STA238H1 / STA248H1 / STA288H1 or equivalents); AND (2) PSY270H1 / PSY270H5 / PSYB57H3 / COG250Y1 (one of). COG250Y1 replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1.

courses.title: Higher Cognitive Processes
courses.description: Advanced topics in cognition including rationality, consciousness, creativity, and human and artificial intelligence.

courses.title: Human Memory
courses.description: Current theories and data on human memory: processes involved in encoding, storage, and retrieval.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites require TWO components: (1) a statistics course (PSY201H1 / ECO220Y1 / STA220H1 / STA238H1 / STA248H1 / STA288H1 or equivalents); AND (2) PSY260H1 / PSYB38H3 OR PSY270H1 / PSY270H5 / PSYB57H3 / COG250Y1. COG250Y1 replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1.

courses.title: Engineering Psychology
courses.description: Applies knowledge of human information processing capabilities to improve human-machine systems design across settings such as aviation, computer software, human-computer interaction, and nuclear power plants.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites require TWO components: (1) a statistics course (PSY201H1 / ECO220Y1 / STA220H1 / STA238H1 / STA248H1 / STA288H1 or equivalents); AND (2) PSY270H1 / COG250Y1 OR PSY280H1. COG250Y1 replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1. Recommended preparation: PSY202H1 (second statistics).

courses.title: Human Memory and Learning Laboratory
courses.description: Examination of issues and methods in human memory research. Students undertake supervised research projects and engage with reading, critiquing, and writing research articles.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites require TWO components: (1) PSY202H1 / ECO220Y1 / SOC252H1 / STA221H1 or equivalents (NOTE: requires second statistics course, not PSY201H1 level); AND (2) PSY260H1 / PSYB38H3 OR PSY270H1 / COG250Y1. COG250Y1 replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1. Recommended preparation: PSY203H1.
courses.verification_note: 36P laboratory format. Limited to 15 students. Priority given to PSY Specialists and Research Specialists during first enrolment period.

courses.title: Vision Science
courses.description: Integrates psychological, neuroscientific, and computational perspectives on vision. Covers history and philosophy of the field, low-level and spatial vision, motion and colour perception, object and scene recognition, attention and consciousness, and multisensory integration. Includes demonstrations and in-class experiments.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites require TWO components: (1) a statistics course (PSY201H1 / ECO220Y1 / STA220H1 / STA238H1 / STA248H1 / STA288H1 or equivalents); AND (2) one of four options: PSY280H1 / PSY270H1+COG250Y1 / PSY290H1+HMB200H1 / COG341H1. COG250Y1 replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1.

courses.title: Moral Development
courses.description: Surveys multiple theoretical frameworks for understanding moral development, including cognitive-developmental, psychoanalytic, sociobiological, behaviouristic, and cultural-anthropological perspectives. Addresses definitions of morality, the link between moral judgement and action, gender-based differences and similarities, and the role of culture in moral development.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites require TWO components: (1) a statistics course (PSY201H1 / ECO220Y1 / STA220H1 / STA238H1 / STA248H1 / STA288H1 or equivalents); AND (2) PSY210H1 / PSY210H5 / PSYB20H3.

courses.title: Attention and Performance
courses.description: Lecture course covering visual cognition and human performance topics, including attention, working memory, spatial cognition, the processing of features and objects, and cognitive motor control related to eye and limb movements.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites require TWO components: (1) a statistics course (PSY201H1 / ECO220Y1 / STA220H1 / STA238H1 / STA248H1 / STA288H1 or equivalents); AND (2) PSY270H1 / COG250Y1 OR PSY280H1. COG250Y1 replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1.

courses.title: Cognitive Neuroscience
courses.description: Capstone lecture course surveying how the mind arises from the brain. Covers how human brain processes — from the firing of a single neuron to the dynamics of billions — support cognitive abilities such as recognizing faces, remembering events, understanding spoken language, and planning routes. Also covers contemporary research methods for engaging with primary literature and understanding ongoing debates.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites require TWO components: (1) a statistics course (PSY201H1 / ECO220Y1 / STA220H1 / STA238H1 / STA248H1 / STA288H1 or equivalents); AND (2) one of three options: PSY260H1 / PSY270H1+COG250Y1 / PSY290H1+HMB200H1. COG250Y1 replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1.

courses.title: Seminar in Cognitive Science
courses.description: Advanced treatment of cognitive science topics such as neuroscientific theories of consciousness, rationality, and modelling of cognitive processes.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites: COG250Y1 (replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1); AND PSY270H1; AND 14.0 credits completed. 36S seminar format.

courses.title: Seminar in Cognitive Science
courses.description: Advanced treatment of cognitive science topics including the application of core ideas from probability theory, information theory, statistics, and machine learning to modelling human cognition and AI.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites are complex with multiple components: (1) 14.0 credits; (2) CSC148H1; (3) COG250Y1 (replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1); (4) COG260H1; (5) a math sequence: (MAT130H1/MAT135H1 + MAT136H1) / (MAT148H1 + MAT149H1) / MAT137Y1; (6) 0.5 credit in statistics. All components must be satisfied. 36S seminar format.

courses.title: Seminar in Cognitive Science
courses.description: Advanced treatment of cognitive science topics including language processes and language representation, production and comprehension, language acquisition, and the interplay of language with theory of mind, pragmatics, reference, and context.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisites: (1) COG250Y1 (replaced by COG100H1 + COG200H1); (2) JLP315H1 / JLP374H1 (one of); (3) LIN232H1 / LIN241H1 (one of); (4) 14.0 credits completed. 36S seminar format.

courses.title: COG Independent Study
courses.description: Advanced independent study course. Requires a formal application with a written proposal and a confirming faculty supervisor. Submissions go to the UC Programs Office for the Program Director's approval. Deadlines fall before the first day of course enrolment. Not eligible for CR/NCR.
courses.prerequisite_note: Official prerequisite: 15.0 credits completed AND a minimum cGPA of 3.0.
courses.verification_note: Breadth requirement not listed on Academic Calendar page. Full-year course (1.0 credit).
